title: Stateline: 17+ Federal Immigration Shooting Incidents Since July 2025; DHS Accounts Contradict Video
excerpt: An investigation by Stateline found at least 17 open-fire incidents involving federal immigration agents since July 2025, with multiple instances where official DHS accounts of self-defense were contradicted by surveillance footage, bystander video, and local law enforcement.
content:
A January 2026 investigation by Stateline — drawing on data compiled by The Trace and other sources — found at least 17 open-fire incidents involving federal immigration agents between July 2025 and January 2026, including fatal shootings, non-fatal shootings, and cases where shots were fired.

In multiple cases, DHS's official accounts of self-defense directly contradicted available evidence. In a December shooting in Glen Burnie, Maryland, DHS revised its account after local police contradicted the initial version. In the fatal shooting of Renee Good in Minneapolis, Minnesota officials stated footage did not support DHS's claim of an imminent threat. In an August incident in San Bernardino, California, agents fired at a family's vehicle three times; DHS claimed justification, but surveillance footage did not show agents being struck by the vehicle.

  • A separate Wall Street Journal investigation found 13 incidents since July in which immigration agents fired at or into civilian vehicles
  • DHS and ICE did not respond to Stateline's multiple requests for comment on discrepancies between official accounts and publicly available evidence
  • Use-of-force experts cited the pattern as evidence of inadequate training for community-based enforcement operations
"I can't think of another time in my lifetime — I'm 50 years old — where we've seen this sort of force in the streets" — immigration policy expert César Cuauhtémoc García Hernández
